title: Bhooth Bangla BO: Akshay Kumar starrer marches towards Rs 100 crore in week 1
description: 'Bhooth Bangla', directed by Priyadarshan and starring Akshay Kumar, Tabu, Paresh Rawal, Wamiqa Gabbi, Rajpal Yadav, Mithila Palkar and Asrani, is closing in on the Rs. 100 crore India net milestone as it completes its first week in theatres. With a cumulative India net of Rs. 79.02 crore and a worldwide gross of Rs. 127.37 crore after seven days, the U/A certified horror-comedy has delivered a strong opening week despite competing against 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' in its fifth week and a full IPL 2026 season. Marking Akshay Kumar and Priyadarshan's reunion after 16 years, the 145-minute supernatural thriller follows Arjun Acharya confronting the demonic entity Vadhusur in his ancestral home in Mangalpur, featuring a nostalgic ensemble of Paresh Rawal, Rajpal Yadav and the late Asrani.

description: Iran's consulate in Hyderabad shared an AI-generated video mocking US President Donald Trump's announcement of a ceasefire extension with Iran. The clip humorously depicts a fictional Trump agreeing to the extension at Pakistan's request, following Iran's accusations of US "disregard and lack of good faith" amid the ongoing peace negotiations.

description: Mitchell Starc is set to join Delhi Capitals for IPL 2026 on Friday after Cricket Australia clearances, targeting a May 1 return against Rajasthan Royals. The injured fast bowler's arrival presents a selection dilemma for the Axar Patel-led side, which needs strong performances in upcoming matches to boost playoff hopes. Starc's gradual return will be managed carefully.

Founder of one of Wall Street’s biggest law firms Sullivan & Cromwell sends apology letter to judge after AI 'messes up' court filing; says: We sincerely ...
description: Sullivan & Cromwell has apologized to a Federal judge for AI-driven errors in a bankruptcy case filing. The motion contained fictitious case names, fabricated quotes, and non-existent statutes, attributed to "AI hallucinations." The firm stated it failed to follow its verification protocols, regretting the burden on the court and parties involved.

title: Voodoo outrage: Akola self-styled 'godman' lifts kids with his teeth in unsafe rituals
description: Disturbing videos show a self-styled godman in Akola district subjecting children to unsafe, superstition-driven rituals. Chetan Sunil Mule, known as 'Gulal Shesh Maharaj', has been holding such gatherings for years, claiming to cure illnesses. Authorities have summoned all self-styled godmen in the area following widespread outrage and demands for action.

title: After $6.25 bn 'gift' to 'Trump Accounts', Michael & Susan Dell donate $750 m
description: Michael and Susan Dell have pledged $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This significant donation will focus on integrating AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education. The project, expected to open by 2030, aims to enhance Austin's healthcare capabilities and advance medical innovation.

title: Aluminium foil on door handles: Trending US hack but is it smart?
description: A viral 2026 home hack involves wrapping door handles in aluminium foil across the US homes, claiming to reduce static shocks and deter intruders. While foil's conductivity can disperse electrical charges, its security benefits are primarily psychological, offering minimal actual protection. Experts advise it is a supplementary trick, not a substitute for robust security measures.

title: 'We are installing tracking software in your machines': Meta to US employees
description: Meta is installing new tracking software on US employees' computers to capture mouse movements, clicks, and keystrokes for AI training. This initiative aims to improve AI agents' ability to perform work tasks autonomously, like using shortcuts. Meta assures employees the data won't be used for performance reviews, only for enhancing AI models.
